flow_yaml:
name: my-first-flow
description: Simple two-step fetch and summarize

steps:
  fetch:
    run: |
      echo '{"data": "hello world"}'
    outputs: [data]

  summarize:
    run: |
      echo "{\"summary\": \"processed: $data\"}"
    inputs:
      data: fetch.data
    outputs: [summary]

confusion_points.description: No minimal 2-step hello-world in docs — quickstart jumps to a 4-step code-review with agent/human executors
confusion_points.how_resolved: The code-review example in quickstart.md still shows `run:` shorthand and `inputs:` syntax clearly enough to extrapolate a simpler flow. examples/report-test-failure.flow.yaml is a pure-script flow.

confusion_points.description: How script executor passes inputs as env vars ($data in run: block) not explicitly called out in quickstart — only shown by example
confusion_points.how_resolved: Welcome FLOW.yaml uses $feature, $plan in shell scripts, showing inputs become shell variables. README code-review example also uses $diff_summary in prompt fields. Inferred the same applies to `run:` blocks.

confusion_points.description: `stepwise validate` not mentioned in quickstart — quickstart goes straight to `stepwise run`
confusion_points.how_resolved: Found `validate` in README CLI table and confirmed via `stepwise --help`

evidence.requirement: README or docs explain how to create a flow file
evidence.evidence: quickstart.md 'Create your own flow' section (line 29) walks through creating a .flow.yaml with full YAML example and per-step explanations. README lines 57-101 also show a complete inline code-review.flow.yaml.

quickstart.md shows `diff_summary: gather-context.diff_summary` (step.field syntax) and line 80 explains 'Dependencies are implicit from inputs'. README line 164 shows `--var repo_path=...` for $job inputs.

quickstart.md shows `outputs: [diff_summary, commits]` and explains 'Runs shell commands, parses JSON from stdout' — keys in stdout JSON match the outputs list. Pattern consistent across all examples.

evidence.requirement: stepwise validate is documented as a way to check flows
evidence.evidence: README CLI table line 132: 'stepwise validate <flow>   Check a flow for errors'. Also discoverable via `stepwise --help`. Note: quickstart.md does NOT mention validate — minor gap.

evidence.requirement: At least one complete example flow is available to reference
evidence.evidence: flows/welcome/FLOW.yaml is a comprehensive 351-line demo. examples/ has 4 additional flow files (report-test-failure, report-test-linear, report-test-loop, report-test-parallel). README and quickstart both contain inline complete examples.

evidence.requirement: The flow built validates and would run correctly
evidence.evidence: `uv run stepwise validate my-first-flow.flow.yaml` returned '✓ my-first-flow.flow.yaml (2 steps)' with no errors or warnings.
